title: IBBI Strengthens CIRP Rules for Enhanced Transparency in Resolution Process
content:
# IBBI Strengthens CIRP Rules for Enhanced Transparency in Resolution Process

Ek important update hai! The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (IBBI) ne Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) ke rules tighten kar diye hain, with a special focus on boosting transparency in resolution decisions.

Here are the key highlights from the recent amendments:

*   **Expanded CoC Representation:** Ab, Committee of Creditors (CoC) meetings mein eighteen largest unrelated operational creditors ko include karna mandatory hoga. Agar eighteen se kam hain, toh sabhi unrelated operational creditors ko shaamil kiya jayega. This ensures broader representation and diversified viewpoints in crucial decisions.
*   **Observer Role for Operational Creditors:** A new Regulation 16E introduces a provision where, if creditors other than scheduled banks or public financial institutions hold more than 66% voting share, the five largest unrelated operational creditors (including the three largest statutory authorities with statutory dues) ko CoC meetings mein observer ke roop mein invite kiya jayega. They won't have voting rights, but their observations will be recorded in the minutes.
*   **Mandatory Going Concern Assessment Report:** Resolution professional ko ab ek Going Concern Assessment Report prepare karna hoga, detailing estimated income, expenditure, cash flows, working capital needs, aur risks of value erosion. Based on this report, CoC decide karegi ki corporate debtor ke operations continue honge ya nahi.
*   **Prior Approval for Insolvency Costs:** All insolvency resolution process costs incurred till the first CoC meeting, along with proper justification, must be placed before the CoC for approval. After the first meeting, all such costs will require prior approval from the committee.
*   **Detailed Justification for Resolution Plans:** CoC ko ab resolution plans ke feasibility, viability, expected recovery vis-a-vis fair and liquidation values, aur market discovery ki adequacy par apni detailed deliberations record karni hongi. This aims to bring more accountability in the approval process.

These amendments, notified via the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (Insolvency Resolution Process for Corporate Persons) (Fourth Amendment) Regulations, 2026, with F.No. IBBI/2026-27/GN/REG153, dated June 8, 2026, are a significant step towards strengthening transparency, stakeholder participation, and overall accountability in the CIRP framework.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://egazette.nic.in/WriteReadData/2026/273309.pdf)

*Published on: 12 June 2026*
